mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits faster lower discount rates and easier financial conditions into equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then reflect accommodation rather than recession stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is flat to falling.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, equity markets price weaker earnings and higher recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The signal is most reliable when PAYEMS is also slowing, confirming that labor deterioration is broad rather than a one-off spike.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal remains negative when PAYEMS growth is strong.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates firmer nominal spending and better revenue visibility for U.S. firms, which supports S&P 500 prices over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when sales growth is driven only by inflation without real activity support.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are not higher following 3-month RSAFS acceleration, or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is accelerating faster than RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals improving industrial throughput and stronger cyclically sensitive earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is stronger when HOUST is also rising, because broad real-sector demand is then validating the industrial pickup.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly increases in INDPRO do not correspond to above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when HOUST is falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST signals housing demand recovery and a coming improvement in construction-related activity, which markets discount into equities with a lag. This should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when broader employment is stable.
failure_condition: Reject if high-HOUST triggers are followed by non-positive 60d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal fails when UNRATE is not worsening.

mechanism_narrative: A low but stable FEDFUNDS rate near zero supports equities by anchoring discount rates and easing refinancing pressure, creating a positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect fails if the low rate coincides with accelerating unemployment or falling industrial production, which would indicate stress rather than accommodation.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% do not outperform baseline over 5 days, especially when UNRATE is rising or INDPRO is falling.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS trend indicates labor-market stabilization and improving household income expectations, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is negative when payrolls are still contracting year over year, because that implies earnings risk dominates.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not better after PAYEMS inflection higher, or if the effect is absent when UNRATE is already falling.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary recovery, rising RSAFS combined with flat or falling CPIAUCSL indicates real demand gains rather than pure price pressure, which should be bullish for the S&P 500 over 20 trading days. If sales rise only because CPIAUCSL is accelerating, the signal fails because margins are being squeezed.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction does not improve 20d SPX returns versus either series alone, or if the effect reverses when CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds sales growth.
